message:
fix: handle poisoned RwLock in FD audit and validate WezTerm FT block markers

Two robustness improvements to frankenterm-core:

fd_budget.rs:
- Recover gracefully from a poisoned audit_history RwLock instead of
  panicking — if a prior thread panicked while holding the write lock,
  the audit method now logs a warning, clears the history via
  into_inner(), and continues with a fresh leak-detection window
- Add test that poisons the lock via catch_unwind and verifies that
  subsequent audit() calls succeed without panic

setup.rs:
- Extract validated_wezterm_ft_block_bounds() to validate FT marker
  structure before creating a backup or modifying the config file
- Detect and return descriptive errors for three corruption cases:
  begin marker without end marker, end marker without begin marker,
  and markers in reversed order
- Refactor unpatch_wezterm_config_at() to use the new validator,
  eliminating two unwrap() calls (previously marked ubs:ignore) and
  ensuring backups are only created after marker structure is confirmed
  valid
- Add three tests covering each partial/reversed marker error case,
  verifying the original file is left untouched on error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
